response: The global food system is under severe strain, as a projected 2050 global population of 9.7 billion will require a 60% rise in food production, while concurrent threats include climate-related yield declines, agriculture accounting for 25% of global greenhouse gas emissions, water depletion in major farming regions, soil degradation affecting 33% of arable land, and one-third of all produced food being wasted. A range of solutions are being considered to address this crisis, including AI-enabled precision agriculture, vertical farming, alternative proteins, drought-resistant crop modification, regenerative farming practices, and policy reforms to subsidies, trade rules and carbon pricing. The central remaining challenge is implementing these solutions at the necessary scale, while also ensuring food remains affordable and accessible for the world's poorest communities.
